evals.prompt: Here is a folder of pre-downloaded HTML pages for an API. Harvest them into clean Markdown and an OpenAPI 3.1 spec.
evals.expected_output: The skill locates or bootstraps the engine, writes a static_html site config, runs the pipeline, and produces out/<site>/{markdown/, models.json, openapi.yaml, checks-report.json} with the validator passing.
assertions: A site config is created from config/_template.yaml rather than editing engine scripts directly
openapi.yaml passes openapi-spec-validator (checks-report.json ok is true)
Engine/config edits are shown as a diff and confirmed before applying

evals.prompt: This API doc site is JavaScript-rendered; plain fetch returns an empty shell. Extract the real content.
evals.expected_output: The skill prefers discovering a content_api endpoint (one-time, via the Network tab procedure in the runbook) and falls back to static_html or rendered only when necessary, then runs the pipeline to produce Markdown + OpenAPI.
assertions: The skill tries content_api before resorting to a headless browser
The chosen acquisition strategy is recorded in the site config
It does not drive a browser to read every page with the model

evals.prompt: The checks-report.json shows ok=false with some failures. Fix the pipeline so output is green.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads the loop runbook, classifies each failure with the signal->fix table, proposes the minimal change to a script or config, shows the diff, gets approval, applies, and re-runs until green.
assertions: Each failure is classified before any edit
The proposed fix is minimal and targeted (config first, script only if needed)
Diffs are shown and approved before applying
After convergence, verified Markdown is frozen into golden/<site>/ and a CHANGELOG line is appended

evals.prompt: Generate the OpenAPI but also figure out the enum values for the status fields from the prose.
evals.expected_output: The baseline OpenAPI is produced deterministically (enums kept in description, not auto-guessed), and enum refinement is offered as an optional model-assisted follow-up rather than silently inferred.
assertions: Enums are not auto-guessed from prose in the baseline spec
Enum refinement is offered explicitly as an optional pass

evals.prompt: The docs have a field typo and an example that disagrees with the parameter table. Just harvest them.
evals.expected_output: The skill harvests faithfully, preserving documentation inconsistencies rather than silently 'fixing' them, and surfaces the inconsistencies to the user at delivery.
assertions: Documentation inconsistencies are preserved, not silently corrected
The inconsistencies are reported to the user at handoff

evals.prompt: The harvest finished and checks pass, but the openapi.yaml is hard to read. Give me something browsable.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates a self-contained out/<site>/openapi.html next to the spec via `npx @redocly/cli build-docs` (offering Scalar's local-server try-it console as the alternative), without modifying the engine, and treats any generation failure as a non-blocking note with the manual command handed over.
assertions: openapi.html is generated beside out/<site>/openapi.yaml using Redocly build-docs by default
If HTML generation fails (no Node/npx, network), the run's success verdict is unaffected and the manual command is provided
No engine script or output artifact is hand-edited to accomplish this
